name: PayU Latin America Payment Processing API
what_it_does: PayU Latin America payment processing REST API for merchants, e-commerce platforms, and marketplaces across Latin America to accept local payment methods, credit cards, bank transfers, and cash payments across Colombia, Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, Chile, Peru, and Panama — enabling multi-currency LATAM payment acceptance with deep local payment method integration including PSE (Colombia), OXXO (Mexico), Boleto (Brazil), Rapipago (Argentina), and Pago Fácil (Argentina) through PayU's regional payment infrastructure. Enables AI agents to manage checkout for LATAM multi-country payment automation, handle payment authorization for credit card and local method processing automation, access refund for LATAM payment reversal automation, retrieve transaction status for payment verification automation, manage recurring payment for subscription billing automation, handle tokenization for card storage and future charge automation, access settlement for LATAM merchant disbursement automation, retrieve reporting for LATAM transaction analytics automation, manage dispute for chargeback response automation, and integrate PayU with LATAM e-commerce, marketplace, and subscription platforms for regional payment automation.
use_cases: Accepting local LATAM payment methods (PSE, OXXO, Boleto) for regional e-commerce checkout automation agents
Processing credit card payments across Colombia, Mexico, Brazil, and Argentina for LATAM merchant automation agents
Managing subscription billing and recurring payments for LATAM SaaS and streaming platform automation agents
Handling LATAM payment disputes, refunds, and chargebacks for merchant operations automation agents
not_for: Outside Latin America (PayU LATAM covers 7 LATAM countries, not global)
Crypto or decentralized payments (PayU is traditional payment gateway)
Real-time gross settlement (PayU is merchant payment gateway, not central bank RTGS)
best_when: A Latin American merchant, e-commerce platform, or global company expanding to LATAM wanting AI agents to accept local payment methods, process credit cards, and handle regional payment complexity across Colombia, Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, Chile, Peru, and Panama.
avoid_when: COUNTRY-SPECIFIC API ENDPOINTS: PayU has separate API endpoints per country (Colombia, Mexico, Brazil, etc.); automated single unified LATAM endpoint assumption creates country_not_found; automated must route to country-specific PayU endpoint. LOCAL PAYMENT METHODS REQUIRE REDIRECT: Cash payment methods (OXXO, Boleto) and bank transfers (PSE) require consumer redirect to payment portal; automated server-side cash payment capture creates flow violation; automated must implement payment method-specific redirect flows. MERCHANT ACCOUNT IS COUNTRY-SPECIFIC: PayU merchant accounts are issued per country; automated single account multi-country assumption creates unauthorized_country; automated must have separate PayU account for each LATAM country of operation. CURRENCY IS LOCAL: Each PayU country processes in local currency (COP, MXN, BRL, ARS, CLP, PEN, USD-Panama); automated USD-only pricing assumption creates currency_not_supported; automated must handle country-specific currencies.

auth.notes: PayU uses API key + merchant ID for authentication. REST API with XML/JSON. Bogotá, Colombia HQ (LATAM headquarters). Founded 2002. Subsidiary of Prosus (Naspers). Products: Payment gateway, tokenization, recurring billing, anti-fraud, local payment methods, settlement. SDKs: JS, PHP, Python, Java, C#. Countries: Colombia, Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, Chile, Peru, Panama. Local methods: PSE, OXXO, Boleto, Rapipago, Pago Fácil, Efecty, Via Baloto. Competes with MercadoPago and Kushki for LATAM payments.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sandbox environment; production requires merchant agreement per country
pricing.paid_tiers: MDR per transaction (varies by country and payment method); monthly fixed fees possible
pricing.notes: Bogotá CO. Prosus/Naspers subsidiary. MDR per country/method. 7 LATAM countries. Local payment methods.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key per country; payment authorization APIs throttled

known_agent_gotchas: COUNTRY-SPECIFIC ENDPOINTS: PayU has separate API base URLs per country; automated single endpoint assumption creates routing error; automated must configure country-specific endpoint (Colombia: api.payulatam.com/payments-api/4.0/service, Mexico: similar country suffix)

SEPARATE MERCHANT ACCOUNTS PER COUNTRY: PayU issues separate merchant ID and API key per country of operation; automated single credential assumption creates unauthorized_country; automated must manage separate credential sets for each LATAM market

LOCAL PAYMENT METHODS REQUIRE REDIRECT: OXXO (Mexico), Boleto (Brazil), PSE (Colombia), and Efecty (Colombia) require consumer redirect to payment portal or voucher generation; automated card-only checkout assumption misses 40-70% of LATAM payment volume; automated must implement redirect flows

CURRENCY MUST MATCH COUNTRY: Each country processes in local currency; automated USD pricing creates currency_not_supported; automated must convert prices to COP, MXN, BRL, ARS, CLP, PEN, or USD (Panama) per market

ANTI-FRAUD FIELDS REQUIRED: PayU's anti-fraud system requires buyer IP, device fingerprint, and shipping/billing address; automated minimal payload assumption creates higher decline rates; automated must include all anti-fraud fields for best authorization rates
agent_readiness.security_notes: LATAM payment gateway. Multi-country regulatory. API key. 7 LATAM countries. Card and local payment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: LATAM payments. Multi-country financial regulations. API key. LATAM. Credit card and local payment data.
